title: Countries Meet to Talk About Moving Away from Fossil Fuels
summary: Many countries are meeting in Colombia to talk about stopping the use of coal, oil, and gas. These fuels cause pollution and warm the Earth. The meeting is special because it is the first time countries are talking about completely moving away from these fuels. About 60 countries are there, including Colombia, Australia, and Nigeria. But big countries like the US, China, and India are not part of the talks. The meeting is happening because the United Nations climate meetings have been slow to make decisions. Scientists say the Earth is warming fast, and we need to act quickly. If the Earth gets too warm, we will have more dangerous weather like floods, fires, and heatwaves. The countries at the meeting want to show that many nations support clean energy like solar and wind power. They hope to encourage other countries to also switch to clean energy.

keywords.explanation: Fuels like coal, oil, and gas that come from ancient plants and animals. They are used for energy but cause pollution.
The usual weather in a place over a long time. Climate change means the weather is changing.
Energy sources that can be used again and again, like sunlight and wind.

questions.question: What is the main topic of the meeting in Colombia?
options: How to use more fossil fuels
How to move away from fossil fuels
questions.correct_answer: How to move away from fossil fuels

questions.question: How many countries are at the meeting?
Which big countries are NOT at the meeting?
Why is the meeting happening?
options: Because UN meetings are too fast

questions.question: What do scientists say about the Earth?
What kind of energy do the countries want to use instead of fossil fuels?
options: Clean energy like solar and wind
questions.correct_answer: Clean energy like solar and wind

background_read: Fossil fuels like coal, oil, and gas are used to make electricity, run cars, and heat homes. But when we burn them, they release gases that trap heat in the air and make the Earth warmer. This is called global warming. Many countries are trying to use more clean energy from the sun, wind, and water to reduce pollution and slow down global warming.
Article_Structure: WHO: Around 60 countries including Colombia, Australia, and Nigeria are meeting. Big countries like the US, China, and India are not there.
WHAT: They are meeting to talk about completely moving away from fossil fuels like coal, oil, and gas.
WHERE: The meeting is in Santa Marta, Colombia.
WHY: Because UN climate meetings have been slow to make decisions, and scientists say the Earth is warming fast, so we need to act quickly.

perspectives.description: They think moving away from fossil fuels is important to stop global warming.
Big countries like the US, China, and India are not at the meeting, so their views are not represented.
